messages.content:
Retailers may utilize different branding strategies to get the advantage of loyal customers. In the examples below, which of the following branding strategies have been adopted?
" 			 				 					 						Tesco Extra, Tesco (Superstores), Tesco Metro, Tesco Express" -  "Gap, GapKids, babyGap, GapMaternity, Gap- Body, Banana Republic, Old Navy, Athleta"
 					 				 			 		
 					 				 			 		
(10 Puan)


Family brands - Umbrella Brand

Umbrella brand - Mixed Strategy

Mixed strategy - Family Brands

The first example, Tesco, utilizes a mixed branding strategy with different store formats being offered under the parent brand. The second example, Gap Inc., uses a family branding strategy with multiple brand names falling under the same corporate entity.

Which of the followings is not an example of temporary price reduction?
(5 Puan)


“10 % extra for the same price” or “buy one, get one free”

a) Multipack products

b) Single product

C)Coupons

Multipack products are not an example of temporary price reduction. Multipack products usually offer a lower price per unit by buying a larger quantity together, but the price reduction is not temporary. The other options, such as "10 % extra for the same price" or "buy one, get one free," single product price reductions, and coupons all represent temporary price reductions as they offer a discount for a limited time or a limited quantity.

The highest level of channel integration, ______________________, involves total channel integration. Customers can shop via all retail channels in parallel, because this system is designed as a holistic entity, ideally with every touch- point of every retail channel available. Customers can therefore combine channels as they see fit, depending on their specific needs. For example, they can use the re- tailer’s shopping app on their smartphones while shopping at the retailer’s brick-and- mortar outlet to scan product barcodes and collect additional information via mobile Internet.


a) Omni-channel

b) Multi-channel

c) Cross channel

d) Multiple channel
a) Omni-channel

Which of the following is not a function of retailers?
(5 Puan)


Breaking Bulk

Bridging Space and Time

Carrying out Transactions

Bargaining with manufacturers

Bargaining with manufacturers is not a function of retailers. The primary functions of retailers are breaking bulk, bridging space and time, carrying out transactions, and providing assortments. Retailers negotiate with manufacturers and suppliers to obtain products to provide assortments to customers, but bargaining with manufacturers is not considered a separate function of retailers.

Retailers can follow several strategies for expansion. Franchising is an encouraging method for rapid growth. Which of the followings is not an advantage of franchisor?
(5 Puan)


Written franchise agreements require franchisees to adhere to stringent operating rules set by the franchisor.

Franchisees finance the investment for establishing stores.

Franchisees have knowledge of local markets and customer and employee contact is direct and personal, so they usually develop close relationships with local customers

Franchisees can ally to restrict the influence of the franchisor and attempt to change the rules.

Franchisees can ally to restrict the influence of the franchisor and attempt to change the rules is not an advantage of franchisors, but rather a potential disadvantage. The other options are advantages of franchising, as written franchise agreements ensure consistency and adherence to company standards, franchisees finance their own investment allowing for rapid expansion without the need for large amounts of capital, and franchisees' local knowledge and personal touch can lead to strong relationships with customers. However, if franchisees band together to challenge the franchisor, it can lead to conflict and potential impact on the system as a whole.

In __________________ pricing, a retailer identifies its main rivals in the market and sets prices accordingly.
(5 Puan)


Competition-based pricing

Demand-oriented pricing

Cost-based pricing

Value-based pricing
Competition-based pricing.

IKEA changed its business model from “do it yourself” to “do it for you”, now often provides the customer with craftspeople who assemble the product bought at their stores.
Such a change in IKEA'a business model will result in ______________________.


(5 Puan)


an increase in customer complaint

an increase inCustomer dissatisfaction

an increase in sales and customer loyalty

an increase in internal customer satisfaction

an increase in sales and customer loyalty. By providing craftspeople to assemble products purchased from their stores, IKEA is offering more convenience and a better customer experience, which can lead to increased sales and customer loyalty. This shift in their business model could improve their reputation and image, enhancing their relationships with customers and ultimately boosting sales.

refers to a corporate strategy that uses the branding concept to build an image of the company as an attractive place to work. Thus, the objective is to differentiate the retailer from the competition in the labour market.
 					 				 			 		
(5 Puan)


Employer branding

Store branding
Employer branding.

Retailers may adopt several pricing strategies based on their retail formats. With _________________ pricing strategy, retailers have relatively high regular prices, but use substantial temporary price reductions to advertise their products, draw customers into stores and increase customer traffic & impulsive buying.
Promotional pricing.

2007, the Swiss retailer Migros bought a 70 % stake in the Swiss discount chain Denner, adding 470 outlets to its store network. This is an example of________________.
(5 Puan)


Merger

Acquisition

Joint venture

Franchising
Acquisition.

Which of the followings is an example of market development as a growth strategy?
(5 Puan)


Amazon expanding its product assortment from media products to electronics, fashion and many other categories.

AmazonBusiness in spring 2015 to tap the business customer segment with a specific assortment, pricing and even business-specific procurement processes (including multi-user accounts, order approval workflows, etc.)

AmazonBusiness in spring 2015 to tap the business customer segment with a specific assortment, pricing and even business-specific procurement processes (including multi-user accounts, order approval workflows, etc.) is an example of market development strategy as it involves expanding the target market to a new customer group (business customers) while still operating in the same product/market space.

Retailers may expand their operations into international markets. To decide which market to enter depends on the data related to the economic, socio demographic, cultural, legal and technological environment of the target market. Please rank the entry modes (greenfield investment, joint venture, exporting, acquisition, franchising) from the least risky to the riskiest.
(15 Puan)

Ranking the entry modes from the least risky to the riskiest, based on market entry risk:

1. Exporting - This entry mode involves the least risk and investment as the retailer sells goods from its home country into international markets, avoiding the need for physical infrastructure or investment in a foreign location.

2. Franchising - Franchising involves the retailer granting the right to use its brand name, products, and business processes to a local partner who operates the business under the retailer's guidelines. This approach shares operational risk and investment costs with the local partner, reducing the risk of market entry.

3. Joint venture - A joint venture with a local firm in the target market can help reduce risks related to cultural and legal differences, market knowledge gaps, and other factors. The risk is shared between the parties.

4. Acquisition - Acquiring an existing business in the target market can provide an established customer base and local infrastructure to the retailer. However, it may involve a significant capital investment and may have cultural and regulatory challenges.

5. Greenfield investment - Establishing a new retail operation in a foreign market from scratch has the highest risk and the largest investment commitment. It requires market research and investment for resources such as land, building, and personnel.

Which of the followings is not an example of differentiation tools engaged by the retailers?
(5 Puan)


Complementing the product with value-added services

Designing a regular but easy-to-use store layout

Providing click and collect option to the customers

Adopting everyday low price strategy

Adopting everyday low price strategy. Differentiation strategies are about distinguishing a retailer's offering from competitors and can be achieved through various means such as complementary services, unique store layouts, or convenient features like click-and-collect. Everyday low pricing, however, is a low-cost strategy rather than differentiation, as it focuses on offering consistently low prices to consumers rather than offering unique and differentiated products or services.

_______________________ are the stores which are located in easy-to-access locations, such as heavily frequented areas or urban neighbourhood locations. They open long hours (up to 24 hours, depending on local or national legislation). The stores are small and facilities are limited, with an average atmosphere and average service level. They can be operated as standalone units (e. g., Tesco Express, 7-Eleven, Auchan, Coop Pronto), but are often coupled with petrol stations (e. g., Shell Shops, BP or Aral Stores, Esso Shops). The focus of those stores is on ease of shopping and they serve food oriented merchandise with a very limited assortment.
(5 Puan)


Department store

Convenience Store

Hypermarket

Specialty store
Convenience Store.

A ____________________________ is formed when two or more parties decide to undertake economic activity together and create a new enterprise as a legal entity in order to pursue a set of agreed goals. The parties agree to contribute equity and share the revenue, expenses and control of the enterprise. For example, when the Spanish Inditex group entered Germany in 1998, it did so in a 50:50 partnerhip with the German Otto group.
Joint venture.

Diversification involves offering new products to new markets and includes several sub-strategies: horizontal diversification, vertical diversification and conglomerate diversification. Which of the following is a vertical diversification example?
 					 				 			 		
(5 Puan)


Migros has opened facilities to produce fresh meat 

The German food retailer REWE now operates home improvement stores (toom)

Migros, Şok and A101 have provided banking services to their customers via money transfers or credit sal

Migros has opened facilities to produce fresh meat is an example of vertical diversification as it involves expanding the business into a new stage in the current product's value chain. In this case, Migros expanded its operations vertically in the meat industry by producing fresh meat. The other options, REWE operating home improvement stores (toom) and Migros, Şok, and A101 providing banking services to customers, are examples of horizontal diversification and conglomerate diversification, respectively.

refers to a corporate strategy that uses the branding concept to build an image of the company as an attractive place to work. Thus, the objective is to differentiate the retailer from the competition in the labour market.
 					 				 			 		
(5 Puan)


Employer branding

Store branding
Employer branding.

At global scale Walmart has had the highest sales volume for years. Its success can be observed through the Global Powers of Retailing reports since 2006. Walmart, with its reasonable prices, attracts mostly the price-sensitive consumers. Its logistics system also supports its pricing strategy. In its __________________ pricing strategy, prices remain stable over long periods.
Everyday low pricing.
